description:
You are an IT-support field technician who has just received a ticket labelled
    “Loopback Interface Sanity Check”.  The customer wants written proof that three
    basic network-diagnostic commands all succeed on the local machine.

    1. Create a new directory  
       /home/user/network_diagnostics  

    2. Inside that directory create a plain-text log file named  
       diagnostics.log

    3. Run exactly the following three diagnostics, in the order listed
       (do **not** add extra flags or different hosts):

       • ping -c 2 127.0.0.1  
       • traceroute -m 5 127.0.0.1  
       • nslookup localhost

    4. After each command finishes, write a single log-line to
       /home/user/network_diagnostics/diagnostics.log
       using the precise, three-column, pipe-delimited format shown
       below (one space on each side of the “|” symbol, terminal newline at the
       end of every log line):

       COMMAND | STATUS | NOTE

       where
       • COMMAND  is the exact command string you executed
       • STATUS   is literally the word “SUCCESS” if the command
                   returned an exit status of 0, otherwise “FAIL”
       • NOTE     must always be the fixed string “loopback_check”

       Important: use the exact command text (no extra spaces), the
       uppercase keywords “SUCCESS”/“FAIL”, and the fixed note
       “loopback_check”.  Do NOT add a timestamp or any other data.

       Example of the final file after all three diagnostics run (this
       is exactly what the automated grading logic will expect, including
       the final newline):

       ping -c 2 127.0.0.1 | SUCCESS | loopback_check
       traceroute -m 5 127.0.0.1 | SUCCESS | loopback_check
       nslookup localhost | SUCCESS | loopback_check

    5. Double-check that /home/user/network_diagnostics/diagnostics.log
       contains exactly three lines, in the precise order above, with a
       newline character at the end of the last line.

    Once the log file is ready, your work is complete.
truth:
Expected directory:
      /home/user/network_diagnostics  (must exist, permissions 755)

    Expected file:
      /home/user/network_diagnostics/diagnostics.log  (permissions 644)

    Expected exact file content (including final trailing newline):

ping -c 2 127.0.0.1 | SUCCESS | loopback_check
traceroute -m 5 127.0.0.1 | SUCCESS | loopback_check
nslookup localhost | SUCCESS | loopback_check

    The automated test will:
      1. Verify directory existence & permissions.
      2. Verify file existence & permissions.
      3. Read diagnostics.log and compare byte-for-byte with the
         content block above.
